name: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle
body_text: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Rubric (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: In the darkness, fire is kindled; after which the Celebrant may address the people in these or similar words
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Text (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: Dear friends in Christ: On this most holy night, in which our Lord Jesus passed over from death to life, the Church invites her members, dispersed throughout the world, to gather in vigil and prayer. For this is the Passover of the Lord, in which, by hearing his Word and celebrating his Sacraments, we share in his victory over death.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Rubric (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: The Celebrant may say the following prayer
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Text (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: Let us pray.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Text (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: O God, through your Son you have bestowed upon your people the brightness of your light: Sanctify this new fire, and grant that in this Paschal feast we may so burn with heavenly desires, that with pure minds we may attain to the festival of everlasting light; through Jesus Christ our Lord. Amen.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Rubric (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: The Paschal Candle is then lighted from the newly kindled fire, and the Deacon (the Celebrant if there is no deacon) bearing the Candle, leads the procession to the chancel, pausing three times and singing or saying
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: All (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: The light of Christ.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle
raw_metadata.speaker: All

name: People (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: Thanks be to God.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle
raw_metadata.speaker: People

name: Rubric (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: If candles have been distributed to members of the congregation, they are lighted from the Paschal Candle at this time. Other candles and lamps in the church, except for those at the Altar, may also be lighted.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Rubric (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: The Paschal Candle is placed in its stand.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Rubric (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: Then the Deacon, or other person appointed, standing near the Candle, sings or says the Exsultet, as follows (the indicated sections may be omitted)
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Text (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: Rejoice now, heavenly hosts and choirs of angels, and let your trumpets shout Salvation for the victory of our mighty King.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Text (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: Rejoice and sing now, all the round earth, bright with a glorious splendor, for darkness has been vanquished by our eternal King.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Text (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: Rejoice and be glad now, Mother Church, and let your holy courts, in radiant light, resound with the praises of your people.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Optional Block (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: All you who stand near this marvelous and holy flame, pray with me to God the Almighty for the grace to sing the worthy praise of this great light; through Jesus Christ his Son our Lord, who lives and reigns with him, in the unity of the Holy Spirit, one God, for ever and ever. Amen.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: All (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: The Lord be with you.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle
raw_metadata.speaker: All

name: Answer (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: And also with you.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle
raw_metadata.speaker: Answer

name: Deacon (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: Let us give thanks to the Lord our God.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle
raw_metadata.speaker: Deacon

name: Answer (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: It is right to give him thanks and praise.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle
raw_metadata.speaker: Answer

name: Rubric (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: Deacon
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Text (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: It is truly right and good, always and everywhere, with our
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Text (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: whole heart and mind and voice, to praise you, the invisible, almighty, and eternal God, and your only-begotten Son, Jesus Christ our Lord; for he is the true Paschal Lamb, who at the feast of the Passover paid for us the debt of Adam's sin, and by his blood delivered your faithful people.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Text (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: This is the night, when you brought our fathers, the children of Israel, out of bondage in Egypt, and led them through the Red Sea on dry land.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Text (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: This is the night, when all who believe in Christ are delivered from the gloom of sin, and are restored to grace and holiness of life.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Text (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: This is the night, when Christ broke the bonds of death and hell, and rose victorious from the grave.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Optional Block (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: How wonderful and beyond our knowing, O God, is your mercy and loving-kindness to us, that to redeem a slave, you gave a Son.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Optional Block (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: How holy is this night, when wickedness is put to flight, and sin is washed away. It restores innocence to the fallen, and joy to those who mourn. It casts out pride and hatred, and brings peace and concord.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Optional Block (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: How blessed is this night, when earth and heaven are joined and man is reconciled to God.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Text (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: Holy Father, accept our evening sacrifice, the offering of this candle in your honor. May it shine continually to drive away all darkness. May Christ, the Morning Star who knows no setting, find it ever burning--he who gives his light to all creation, and who lives and reigns for ever and ever. Amen.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: Rubric (The Lighting of the Paschal Candle)
body_text: It is customary that the Paschal Candle burn at all services from Easter Day through the Day of Pentecost.
section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

raw_metadata.section: The Lighting of the Paschal Candle

name: The Liturgy of the Word
body_text: The Liturgy of the Word

name: Rubric (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: The Celebrant may introduce the Scripture readings in these or similar words

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: Let us hear the record of God's saving deeds in history, how he saved his people in ages past; and let us pray that our God will bring each of us to the fullness of redemption.

name: Rubric (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: At least two of the following Lessons are read, of which one is always the Lesson from Exodus. After each Lesson, the Psalm or Canticle listed, or some other suitable psalm, canticle, or hymn may be sung. A period of silence may be kept; and the Collect provided, or some other suitable Collect, may be said.

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: Let us pray. (Silence)

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: O God, who wonderfully created, and yet more wonderfully restored, the dignity of human nature: Grant that we may share the divine life of him who humbled himself to share our humanity, your Son Jesus Christ our Lord. Amen.

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: Let us pray. (Silence)

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: Almighty God, you have placed in the skies the sign of your covenant with all living things: Grant that we, who are saved through water and the Spirit, may worthily offer to you our sacrifice of thanksgiving; through Jesus Christ our Lord. Amen.

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: Let us pray. (Silence)

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: God and Father of all believers, for the glory of your Name multiply, by the grace of the Paschal sacrament, the number of your children; that your Church may rejoice to see fulfilled your promise to our father Abraham; through Jesus Christ our Lord. Amen.

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: Let us pray. (Silence)

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: O God, whose wonderful deeds of old shine forth even to our own day, you once delivered by the power of your mighty arm your chosen people from slavery under Pharaoh, to be a sign for us of the salvation of all nations by the water of Baptism: Grant that all the peoples of the earth may be numbered among the offspring of Abraham, and rejoice in the inheritance of Israel; through Jesus Christ our Lord. Amen.

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: Let us pray. (Silence)

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: O God, you led your ancient people by a pillar of cloud by day and a pillar of fire by night: Grant that we, who serve you now on earth, may come to the joy of that heavenly Jerusalem, where all tears are wiped away and where your saints for ever sing your praise; through Jesus Christ our Lord. Amen.

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: Let us pray. (Silence)

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: O God, you have created all things by the power of your Word, and you renew the earth by your Spirit: Give now the water of life to those who thirst for you, that they may bring forth abundant fruit in your glorious kingdom; through Jesus Christ our Lord. Amen.

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: Let us pray. (Silence)

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: Almighty and everlasting God, who in the Paschal mystery established the new covenant of reconciliation: Grant that all

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: who are reborn into the fellowship of Christ's Body may show forth in their lives what they profess by their faith; through Jesus Christ our Lord. Amen.

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: Let us pray. (Silence)

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: Almighty God, by the Passover of your Son you have brought us out of sin into righteousness and out of death into life: Grant to those who are sealed by your Holy Spirit the will and the power to proclaim you to all the world; through Jesus Christ our Lord. Amen.

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: Let us pray. (Silence)

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: O God of unchangeable power and eternal light: Look favorably on your whole Church, that wonderful and sacred mystery; by the effectual working of your providence, carry out in tranquillity the plan of salvation; let the whole world see and know that things which were cast down are being raised up, and things which had grown old are being made new, and that all things are being brought to their perfection by him through whom all things were made, your Son Jesus Christ our Lord. Amen.

name: Rubric (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: A homily may be preached after any of the preceding Readings.

name: Rubric (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: Holy Baptism (beginning with the Presentation of the Candidates, page 305, and concluding with the reception of the newly baptized) may be administered here or after the Gospel. Confirmation may also be administered.

name: Rubric (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: In the absence of candidates for Baptism or Confirmation, the Celebrant leads the people in the Renewal of Baptismal Vows, either here or after the Gospel.

name: Rubric (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: The Celebrant may first address the people in these or similar words, all standing

name: Text (The Liturgy of the Word)
body_text: Through the Paschal mystery, dear friends, we are buried with Christ by Baptism into his death, and raised with him to newness of life. I call upon you, therefore, now that our Lenten observance is ended, to renew the solemn promises and vows of Holy Baptism, by which we once renounced Satan and all his works, and promised to serve God faithfully in his holy Catholic Church.

name: The Renewal of Baptismal Vows
body_text: The Renewal of Baptismal Vows

name: Celebrant (The Renewal of Baptismal Vows)
body_text: Do you reaffirm your renunciation of evil and renew your commitment to Jesus Christ?

name: People (The Renewal of Baptismal Vows)
body_text: I do.

name: Celebrant (The Renewal of Baptismal Vows)
body_text: Do you believe in God the Father?

name: People (The Renewal of Baptismal Vows)
body_text: I believe in God, the Father almighty, creator of heaven and earth.

name: Celebrant (The Renewal of Baptismal Vows)
body_text: Do you believe in Jesus Christ, the Son of God?

name: People (The Renewal of Baptismal Vows)
body_text: I believe in Jesus Christ, his only Son, our Lord. He was conceived by the power of the Holy Spirit and born of the Virgin Mary. He suffered under Pontius Pilate, was crucified, died, and was buried. He descended to the dead. On the third day he rose again. He ascended into heaven, and is seated at the right hand of the Father. He will come again to judge the living and the dead.

name: Celebrant (The Renewal of Baptismal Vows)
body_text: Do you believe in God the Holy Spirit?

name: People (The Renewal of Baptismal Vows)
body_text: I believe in the Holy Spirit, the holy catholic Church, the communion of saints, the forgiveness of sins, the resurrection of the body, and the life everlasting.

name: Celebrant (The Renewal of Baptismal Vows)
body_text: Will you continue in the apostles' teaching and fellowship, in the breaking of bread, and in the prayers?

name: People (The Renewal of Baptismal Vows)
body_text: I will, with God's help.

name: Celebrant (The Renewal of Baptismal Vows)
body_text: Will you persevere in resisting evil, and, whenever you fall into sin, repent and return to the Lord?

name: People (The Renewal of Baptismal Vows)
body_text: I will, with God's help.

name: Celebrant (The Renewal of Baptismal Vows)
body_text: Will you proclaim by word and example the Good News of God in Christ?

name: People (The Renewal of Baptismal Vows)
body_text: I will, with God's help.

name: Celebrant (The Renewal of Baptismal Vows)
body_text: Will you seek and serve Christ in all persons, loving your neighbor as yourself?

name: People (The Renewal of Baptismal Vows)
body_text: I will, with God's help.

name: Celebrant (The Renewal of Baptismal Vows)
body_text: Will you strive for justice and peace among all people, and respect the dignity of every human being?

name: People (The Renewal of Baptismal Vows)
body_text: I will, with God's help.

name: Rubric (The Renewal of Baptismal Vows)
body_text: The Celebrant concludes the Renewal of Vows as follows

name: Text (The Renewal of Baptismal Vows)
body_text: May Almighty God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has given us a new birth by water and the Holy Spirit, and bestowed upon us the forgiveness of sins, keep us in eternal life by his grace, in Christ Jesus our Lord. Amen.

name: At the Eucharist
body_text: At the Eucharist

name: Rubric (At the Eucharist)
body_text: The candles at the Altar may now be lighted from the Paschal Candle.

name: Rubric (At the Eucharist)
body_text: One of the following Canticles is then sung. Immediately before the Canticle the Celebrant may say to the people

name: All (At the Eucharist)
body_text: Alleluia. Christ is risen.

name: People (At the Eucharist)
body_text: The Lord is risen indeed. Alleluia.

name: Text (At the Eucharist)
body_text: The Canticles

name: Rubric (At the Eucharist)
body_text: The Celebrant then says

name: All (At the Eucharist)
body_text: The Lord be with you.

name: People (At the Eucharist)
body_text: And also with you.

name: Celebrant (At the Eucharist)
body_text: Let us pray.

name: Rubric (At the Eucharist)
body_text: The Celebrant says one of the following Collects

name: Text (At the Eucharist)
body_text: Almighty God, who for our redemption gave your only- begotten Son to the death of the cross, and by his glorious resurrection delivered us from the power of our enemy: Grant us so to die daily to sin, that we may evermore live with him in the joy of his resurrection; through Jesus Christ your Son our Lord, who lives and reigns with you and the Holy Spirit, one God, now and for ever. Amen.

name: Rubric (At the Eucharist)
body_text: or this

name: Text (At the Eucharist)
body_text: O God, who made this most holy night to shine with the glory of the Lord's resurrection: Stir up in your Church that Spirit of adoption which is given to us in Baptism, that we, being renewed both in body and mind, may worship you in sincerity and truth; through Jesus Christ our Lord, who lives and reigns with you, in the unity of the Holy Spirit, one God, now and for ever. Amen.

name: Rubric (At the Eucharist)
body_text: "Alleluia" may be sung and repeated.

name: Rubric (At the Eucharist)
body_text: Psalm 114, or some other suitable psalm or a hymn may be sung.

name: Rubric (At the Eucharist)
body_text: If a sermon or homily was not preached earlier, it follows here.

name: Rubric (At the Eucharist)
body_text: The Nicene Creed is not used at this service.

name: Rubric (At the Eucharist)
body_text: Holy Baptism, Confirmation, or the Renewal of Baptismal Vows may take place here.

name: Rubric (At the Eucharist)
body_text: The celebration continues with the Prayers of the People.

name: Text (At the Eucharist)
body_text: Preface of Easter
